name: Restream Multi-Platform Streaming API
what_it_does: Multi-platform live streaming service with REST API — enabling creators and businesses to simultaneously broadcast to 30+ platforms (YouTube, Twitch, Facebook, LinkedIn) from a single RTMP stream, with chat aggregation, stream scheduling, VOD storage, and analytics.
use_cases: Automating stream channel configuration and destination management via API
Scheduling live streams across platforms programmatically for content calendars
Pulling unified chat and analytics data from multi-platform broadcasts via API
Integrating Restream stream status and viewer counts into production dashboards
Managing RTMP stream keys and destinations for broadcast automation workflows
not_for: Video conferencing and team collaboration (use Zoom or Teams)
Fully managed live production with browser-based studio (use StreamYard)
best_when: A creator or media brand wants to simultaneously multistream to many platforms from their existing encoder (OBS, Wirecast) and aggregate chat and analytics across platforms.
avoid_when: You need a browser-based production studio (use StreamYard), separate track recording (use Riverside), or only stream to one platform.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow. Scopes for channel read/write, stream management, and analytics. Client ID and secret from Restream developer portal. Token refresh required. Webhook for stream start/stop events. API access requires Professional plan or above.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: basic multistreaming to 2 channels; no API access
pricing.notes: Ukrainian company (Kyiv/Austin). Founded 2015. Raised $50M. 200+ platform integrations. 7M+ users. Used by Fortune 500 companies. Chat aggregation from all platforms in one stream. Analytics across all destinations unified. RTMP and WebRTC ingest. Strong creator economy adoption.

performance.rate_limits: OAuth2 rate limits; streaming infrastructure is separate

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for channels, stream keys, schedules, and analytics. OAuth2 with scopes. Good developer documentation. RTMP stream key management is primary API use case. Stream key rotation should be automated. Destinations must be authenticated in UI before API management. Chat aggregation available via WebSocket.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Channel reads idempotent. Stream schedule reads idempotent. Analytics reads idempotent by date range. Stream key rotation creates new key — previous key invalidated.
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires Professional plan — not available on free plan
Destination platforms must be authenticated in Restream UI before API management
RTMP stream keys are sensitive — rotate after broadcasts and store securely
Chat aggregation uses WebSocket, not REST — different authentication flow
Stream analytics have processing delay — not real-time

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. RTMP stream keys are highly sensitive — unauthorized access means hijacking your live stream. Implement key rotation and secure storage. Platform OAuth tokens stored by Restream — evaluate trust model.
